Class WaitingListDAO

java.lang.Object
DB.DBController
DAO.WaitingListDAO
public class WaitingListDAO extends DBController
Data Access Object (DAO) for managing the restaurant's waiting list. This class handles operations for WaitingListEntry, including FIFO retrieval, tracking entry/exit timestamps, and managing exit reasons. Extends DBController to manage database connectivity.

  • Constructor Details

    • WaitingListDAO

      public WaitingListDAO()

  • Method Details

    • getByConfirmationCode

      public WaitingListEntry getByConfirmationCode(int confirmationCode)
      Retrieves a waiting list entry based on its unique confirmation code.
      Parameters:
      confirmationCode - the unique code assigned to the entry.
      Returns:
      a WaitingListEntry if found, null otherwise.

    • getNextWaitingEntry

      public WaitingListEntry getNextWaitingEntry()
      Returns the next customer in line (FIFO - First In, First Out). Retrieves the oldest entry that has not yet exited the waiting list.
      Returns:
      the oldest active WaitingListEntry, or null if the list is empty.

    • updateExitReasonByConfirmationCode

      public void updateExitReasonByConfirmationCode(int confirmationCode, ExitReason reason)
      Updates the exit reason for an entry that is currently active. Used when a customer is seated or decides to leave the list.
      Parameters:
      confirmationCode - the entry identifier.
      reason - the ExitReason status.

    • getWaitlistSubscriberCountBetween

      public int getWaitlistSubscriberCountBetween(LocalDate start, LocalDate end)
      Counts how many registered subscribers used the waiting list within a specific date range. Useful for marketing and activity reports.
      Parameters:
      start - start date of the range.
      end - end date of the range.
      Returns:
      count of subscriber entries.

    • getWaitingEntriesWithoutExitReason

      public List<WaitingListEntry> getWaitingEntriesWithoutExitReason()
      Retrieves all active entries currently waiting for a table.
      Returns:
      a list of active WaitingListEntry objects.

    • updateExitReason

      public boolean updateExitReason(int confirmationCode, ExitReason exitReason)
      Updates or clears the exit reason for a specific entry.
      Parameters:
      confirmationCode - the entry identifier.
      exitReason - the new reason (or null to clear).
      Returns:
      true if successful.

    • addToWitingList

      public Boolean addToWitingList(WaitingListEntry w)
      Adds a new customer to the waiting list. Automatically captures the current system date and time as the entry timestamp.
      Parameters:
      w - the entry details.
      Returns:
      true if added successfully.

    • getWaitingListBetweenDates

      public List<WaitingListEntry> getWaitingListBetweenDates(LocalDate startDate, LocalDate endDate)
      Fetches history of waiting list entries within a range, ordered chronologically.
      Parameters:
      startDate - the start of the reporting period.
      endDate - the end of the reporting period.
      Returns:
      a list of historical entries.

    • findExistingEntry

      public WaitingListEntry findExistingEntry(Integer userID, LocalDate date, LocalTime time)
      Checks if a specific registered user already has an active spot in the waiting list.
      Parameters:
      userID - the ID of the registered user.
      date - requested date.
      time - requested time slot.
      Returns:
      the existing WaitingListEntry if found, null otherwise.

    • findExistingEntryByContact

      public WaitingListEntry findExistingEntryByContact(String email, String phone, LocalDate date, LocalTime time)
      Searches for an active waiting list entry using contact details (Email or Phone). Used for identifying Guests (non-registered users) in the list.
      Parameters:
      email - contact email.
      phone - contact phone.
      date - requested date.
      time - requested time slot.
      Returns:
      an existing WaitingListEntry if match found, null otherwise.
